taxonomy_v3: Engineering and Development, Data Analysis, Data Scientist
core_skills: Data Analytics, Financial Planning, Analysis, Forecasting, Budgeting
skill_match_scores: Data Analytics:1.0, Financial Planning:0.0, Analysis:1.0, Forecasting:0.0, Budgeting:0.0
must_have_quals: Bachelor's degree required; emphasis in data analytics, data science, computer science, management information systems, finance, statistics or related field, 3-5 years of experience performing data analytics in a financial setting (financial statement analysis or development, forecast analysis, trending analytics, understands key finance metrics, chart of accounts, etc.) with strong focus in Financial Planning and Analysis, Experience and strong understanding of forecasting, budgeting, variance analysis, and financial modeling, Hands on practical experience and proficiency utilizing Microsoft Power BI, Azure Databricks, Databricks SQL and SQL Server, Experience with consumer-level and industry-level data, Ability to synthesize complex data into clear, actionable insights for senior leadership consumption, Experience utilizing cloud-based technologies (preferably Azure) and data lakehouse architecture, Be an excellent communicator and collaborator, engaging with multiple technical and business stakeholders and leaders and be able to translate business questions and analysis into simple briefings for use by various parties, Ability to work independently in a fast-paced, growth-oriented environment without sacrificing quality
preferred_quals: Experience with professional service industry or CPA and advisory firms preferred
core_responsibilities: Develop and maintain data models, reports, and dashboards for FP&A function and firm overall with a focus on reporting forecasting data, budgeting and planning data, and trends, and providing insights for leaders, Own end-to-end forecasting, budgeting, and planning reporting to support FP&A function, Translate business needs into analytics/reporting requirements to support FP&A decisions and workflows, Support overall firm initiatives from an FP&A perspective, Proactively analyze and mine data lakehouse to identify additional insights, data quality concerns, and opportunities for additional data modeling, Support users with ad hoc training, troubleshooting, etc. related to FP&A data and reporting
job_summary: Baker Tilly is a leading advisory, tax and assurance firm, providing clients with a genuine coast-to-coast and global advantage. The Data Analytics Senior Associate will support FP&A with insights, management reports, and analysis for decision-making processes, while developing business-critical analysis and reporting to enable data-driven decisions.
